title: wicked-installer per-CLI install script report
description: The single JSON object each per-CLI install script prints to stdout under --json. Exactly one property matching ^[a-z][a-z0-9-]*Home$ MUST be present (e.g. codexHome, claudeHome, geminiHome). The key is the script's <cli> slug (the SAME lowercase/digit/hyphen character set as the marker `cli` field, ^[a-z][a-z0-9-]*$) followed by the literal `Home`, with NO transformation — so a distinct-config-root slug like `gemini-wicked` (§2.1) yields `gemini-wickedHome`, which is valid. v1 emits only that key plus reports[]; all other top-level keys are v1.1-optional. Consumers parse the LAST complete JSON object on stdout (acquisition child processes may interleave output on non-dry runs). See INTERFACE.md §9.

configDirs.description: v1.1 multi-target CLIs (claude): full list of config dirs written; the *Home key stays the primary target.

skipped.description: true only for install.type manual|binary (manual step noted, not a failure).

version.description: v1.1: staged/acquired product version when known.

heuristic.description: v1.1 uninstall only: removal used v1-marker heuristics rather than an exact files[] manifest.

assets.description: Counts by installed asset type. Taxonomy: skills + mcp + hooks + bins. Only skills is guaranteed present — the codex reference emits it alone. agents/commands are LEGACY keys: no conforming script installs agents or commands (§7.2), so they are absent or always 0, never nonzero; they persist only so a v1 parser that reads them doesn't crash.

bins.description: v1.1 additive: count of verified product binaries.

actions.description: v1.1: auditable per-action outcomes. Makes dry-run (result:planned) and uninstall reversible/inspectable.
